662	theorem isRegularLocalRing_of_isLocalization_atPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_perfectField
663	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [PerfectField k]
664	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra k S]
665	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S]
666	    (q : Ideal S) (hq : q.IsPrime)
667	    (Sq : Type u) [CommRing Sq] [IsLocalRing Sq] [Algebra k Sq] [Algebra S Sq]
668	    [IsScalarTower k S Sq] [IsLocalization.AtPrime Sq q] :
669	    IsRegularLocalRing Sq := by
670	  haveI := hq
671	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing k S
672	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Sq := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing q.primeCompl Sq ‹_›
673	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ : ∃ n, Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k S := by
674	    obtain ⟨ι, σ, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := (inferInstance : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k S).out
675	    exact ⟨P.dimension, P.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension rfl⟩
676	  haveI := hn
677	  -- Kähler package at the localisation (free of rank `n`).
678	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄k]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
679	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule q.primeCompl (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq) :=
680	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map k S Sq q.primeCompl
681	  haveI : Module.Free Sq (Ω[Sq⁄k]) :=
682	    Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sq) (S := q.primeCompl)
683	      (M := Ω[S⁄k]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sq⁄k]) (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq)
684	  have hrank : Module.rank Sq (Ω[Sq⁄k]) = n := by
685	    have h := Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sq q.primeCompl
686	      (KaehlerDifferential.map k k S Sq)
687	    rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
688	      (S := S) n] at h
689	    simpa using h
690	  -- Formal smoothness of `Sq` and of its residue field over `k`.
691	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth S Sq := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization q.primeCompl
692	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k Sq := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp k S Sq
693	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k S := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_finiteType k S
694	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType S Sq := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_isLocalization Sq q.primeCompl
695	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k Sq := Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k S Sq
696	  have hResSurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap Sq (ResidueField Sq)) := by
697	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
698	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
699	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType Sq (ResidueField Sq) :=
700	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective (Algebra.ofId Sq (ResidueField Sq)) hResSurj
701	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType Sq (ResidueField Sq) :=
702	    Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_finiteType Sq (ResidueField Sq)
703	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k (ResidueField Sq) :=
704	    Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k Sq (ResidueField Sq)
705	  -- (the `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` low-priority instance)
706	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth k (ResidueField Sq) := inferInstance
707	  -- Lemma D: the conormal dimension identity at `Sq`.
708	  have hD := finrank_cotangentSpace_add_finrank_kaehler_residueField
709	    (R := k) (Sₘ := Sq) n hrank
710	  -- Lemma B: the trdeg–height inequality at `q`.
711	  obtain ⟨d, hd, hB⟩ :=
712	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_le_trdeg_and_natCast_le_height_add
713	      (k := k) n q hq
714	  -- trdeg monotonicity from `S ⧸ q` into the residue field.
715	  have hmono : Algebra.trdeg k (S ⧸ q) ≤ Algebra.trdeg k (ResidueField Sq) := by
716	    set φ : S →ₐ[k] ResidueField Sq :=
717	      (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom k Sq (ResidueField Sq)).comp
718	        (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom k S Sq) with hφdef
719	    have hφmem : ∀ a : S, φ a = 0 ↔ a ∈ q := by
720	      intro a
721	      simp only [hφdef, AlgHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom']
722	      rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq, IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff]
723	      exact IsLocalization.AtPrime.to_map_mem_maximal_iff Sq q a
724	    have hφker : ∀ a ∈ q, φ a = 0 := fun a ha => (hφmem a).mpr ha
725	    have hinj : Function.Injective (Ideal.Quotient.liftₐ q φ hφker) := by
726	      rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
727	      intro x hx
728	      obtain ⟨r, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
729	      rw [Ideal.Quotient.liftₐ_apply, Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk] at hx
730	      rw [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
731	      exact (hφmem r).mp hx
732	    exact trdeg_le_of_injective _ hinj
733	  -- Lemma C at the residue field.
734	  have hC : Module.rank (ResidueField Sq) (Ω[ResidueField Sq⁄k]) =
735	      Algebra.trdeg k (ResidueField Sq) :=
736	    Algebra.rank_kaehlerDifferential_eq_trdeg k (ResidueField Sq)
737	  -- Ω[κ⁄k] is finite-dimensional (surjective image of the f.d. middle term).

739	    rw [← Module.rank_lt_aleph0_iff, hrank]
740	    exact Cardinal.natCast_lt_aleph0

744	  -- `d ≤ dim_κ Ω[κ⁄k]`.

746	      (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (Ω[ResidueField Sq⁄k]) : ℕ∞) := by
747	    have hcard : (d : Cardinal) ≤

749	      rw [hC]
750	      exact le_trans hd hmono
751	    rw [← Module.finrank_eq_rank] at hcard
752	    exact_mod_cast hcard
753	  -- Conclude via the cotangent criterion.
754	  apply IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim
755	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height q Sq]
756	  have hfinal : (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (CotangentSpace Sq) : ℕ∞) ≤ q.height := by
757	    refine (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right
758	      (k := (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (Ω[ResidueField Sq⁄k]) : ℕ∞))
759	      (by simp)).mp ?_
760	    calc (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (CotangentSpace Sq) : ℕ∞)
761	        + (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (Ω[ResidueField Sq⁄k]) : ℕ∞)
762	        = (n : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hD
763	      _ ≤ q.height + d := hB
764	      _ ≤ q.height + (Module.finrank (ResidueField Sq) (Ω[ResidueField Sq⁄k]) : ℕ∞) := by
765	          gcongr

- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

data.content:
Bundled closed-point form of
  \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}: if \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
  is formally smooth over \(R\), \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) is free of rank \(n\),
  and the structure map \(R \to \kappa\) to the residue field is bijective (as at a
  \(\bar k\)-rational closed point, by the Nullstellensatz), then
  \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 = n\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  Proved directly in Lean: bijectivity upgrades the residue field to formally smooth
  with vanishing differentials, reducing to
  \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
  % NOTE (iter-270 dag-walker): \lean{...} pinned to the TODO placeholder
  % decl name. This 6.C assembly has NO separately-pinnable Lean
  % declaration: its content is the in-body closure pattern of
  % \texttt{isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth} (pinned by
  % \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}), so it cannot re-pin that same
  % decl. The TODO pin gives leandag a resolvable \lean{...} target so the
  % node carries a name; it is NOT a claim that a kernel-clean decl exists.
  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
  regular local ring of Krull dimension
  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
  dimension equals \(1\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
      regular. The scheme-level transfer
      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3

The run-0006 T6 diff in `SubProjects/Albanese` does what the report claims. The Stacks 00TT gap is genuinely closed via a new Serre-free route, and both `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` and `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are honestly sorry-free.

**Buried cheats** — none beyond the 3 sanctioned gaps. All three are genuine named-lemma gaps (full statement present, only the proof body is `sorry`):
- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (sorry at line 1386)
- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (line 1454)
- `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (line 1529)

Nothing weakened or made vacuous. Every deleted decl has zero remaining consumers inside `SubProjects/Albanese` — the only lingering references are in the out-of-scope `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` mirror (a separate project, not in the T6 write-set). 7	
8	\chapter{Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)}
9	\label{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}
10	
11	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.a sub-row of Route A
12	% (the positive-genus arm of \texttt{nonempty\_jacobianWitness}). Per
13	% STRATEGY.md L29 it is the \emph{risk-dominant} A.4 sub-row
14	% ("project-fatal if it stalls"). The chapter has two outputs:
15	% (i) \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} --- Milne Theorem~3.1: a rational map
16	%     from a normal variety to a complete variety is defined on the complement
17	%     of a codimension-\(\geq 2\) closed subset. Specialised below to the
18	%     smooth-variety setting where the prover can phrase it as
19	%     "no codim-1 indeterminacy \(\Rightarrow\) extension". This is one of the
20	%     two inputs to Milne Theorem~3.2 in the sibling chapter
21	%     \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}.
22	% (ii) \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} --- Milne Lemma~3.3: for a
23	%     rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	%     indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension \(1\). This is
25	%     the second input to Milne Theorem~3.2; it consumes nothing from A.4.b.
26	% A bonus output is \texttt{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}: the Weil-divisor
27	% reformulation of (i) that a smooth-surface codim-2 extension is obstructed
28	% exactly by the order of the pulled-back coordinates of \(f\) being negative
29	% at a prime divisor. This is the lemma A.4.a uses to align with the project's
30	% \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} machinery.
31	%
32	% Depth-\(\geq 2\) extension on a smooth surface, used in the proof body of
33	% \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} when reducing the general-codim case to the
34	% normal-DVR-codim-1 case, consumes
35	% \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
36	% \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} (the A.4.b sub-row). The chapter
37	% records the dependency but does not re-prove it.
38	%
39	% The Lean target file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}
40	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
41	% creating it.
42	
43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\texttt{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	
95	\leanok
96	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
97	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
98	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
99	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
100	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
101	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
102	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
103	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
104	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
105	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
106	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
107	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
108	  % \varphi$."
109	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
110	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
111	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
112	  \[
113	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
114	  \]
115	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
116	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
117	  \[
118	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
119	  \]
120	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
121	
122	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
123	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
124	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
125	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
126	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
127	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
128	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
129	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
130	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
131	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
132	\end{definition}
133	
134	
135	\begin{definition}
136	
137	
138	\leanok
139	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
140	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
141	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
142	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:prime_divisor}
143	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
144	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
145	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
146	  % PDF page 22).
147	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
148	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
149	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
150	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
151	  conclusion).}
152	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
153	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
154	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
155	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
156	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
157	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
158	
159	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
160	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
161	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
162	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
163	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
164	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
165	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
166	  is empty) as a single line.
167	
168	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
169	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
170	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
171	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
172	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
173	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), \(\eta \in
174	  \texttt{f.domain}\). The encoding matches the standing \(\mathrm{Order.coheight}\)
175	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
176	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
177	\end{definition}
178	
179	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
180	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
181	
182	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
183	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
184	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
185	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
186	
187	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
188	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
189	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
190	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
191	
192	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
193	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
194	
195	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
196	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
197	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
198	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
199	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
200	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
201	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
202	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
203	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
204	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
205	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
206	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
207	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
208	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
209	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
210	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
211	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
212	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
213	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
214	
215	Stages~1--4 of the chain are recorded below as named substrate lemmas, feeding
216	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}.
217	
218	\begin{lemma}\leanok
219	[Stage 1 --- smooth implies flat at every stalk]
220	  \label{lem:stalkMap_flat_of_smooth}
221	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkMap_flat_of_smooth}
222	  Let \(X \to \Spec(\bar k)\) be a smooth morphism over an algebraically closed
223	  field \(\bar k\). Then for every point \(z \in X\) the induced ring map on
224	  stalks is flat.
225	\end{lemma}
226	
227	\begin{proof}
228	  Proved directly in Lean: the smooth-implies-flat instance composed with the
229	  stalk-map flatness characterisation. A sub-step of
230	  \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}.
231	\end{proof}
232	
233	\begin{lemma}\leanok
234	[Stage 2 --- smooth implies a standard-smooth presentation at a point]
235	  \label{lem:exists_standardSmooth_at_of_smooth}
236	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth}
237	  For a smooth morphism \(X \to \Spec(\bar k)\) and a point \(z \in X\), there
238	  exist affine open neighbourhoods \(U \subseteq \Spec(\bar k)\) and \(V \ni z\)
239	  in \(X\) with \(V \subseteq X^{-1}(U)\) such that the induced ring map
240	  \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)\) is standard smooth (Stacks 00T7).
241	\end{lemma}
242	
243	\begin{proof}
244	  Proved directly in Lean: re-export of the scheme-level Stacks 00T7 packaging.
245	  A sub-step of \cref{lem:exists_algebra_standardSmooth_stalk_localization}.
246	\end{proof}
247	
248	\begin{lemma}\leanok
249	[Stage 3 --- standard-smooth algebra package at the stalk]
250	  \label{lem:exists_algebra_standardSmooth_stalk_localization}
251	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth}
252	  \uses{lem:exists_standardSmooth_at_of_smooth, lem:isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen}
253	  For a smooth morphism \(X \to \Spec(\bar k)\) and a point \(z \in X\), there is
254	  an affine open neighbourhood \(V \ni z\) and an \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, U)\)-algebra
255	  structure on \(\Gamma(X, V)\) making it a standard-smooth algebra, together with a
256	  witness that the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, z}\) is the localisation of \(\Gamma(X, V)\)
257	  at the prime ideal corresponding to \(z\).
258	\end{lemma}
259	
260	\begin{proof}
261	  Proved directly in Lean: composes the Stage-2 presentation
262	  \cref{lem:exists_standardSmooth_at_of_smooth} with the ring-hom-to-algebra bridge
263	  and the affine-open stalk localisation
264	  \cref{lem:isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen}. A sub-step of
265	  \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}.
266	\end{proof}
267	
268	\begin{lemma}\leanok
269	[Stage 4 --- K\"ahler differentials of a standard-smooth algebra are free]
270	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth}
271	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth}
272	  For a standard-smooth \(R\)-algebra \(S\), the module of K\"ahler differentials
273	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module (Stacks 00T7(2)), with basis the family
274	  \(\{ds_i\}\) of the standard-smooth presentation.
275	\end{lemma}
276	
277	\begin{proof}
278	  Proved directly in Lean: from the basis provided by the standard-smooth
279	  presentation. Supplies the freeness hypothesis of
280	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}.
281	\end{proof}
282	
283	\begin{lemma}
284	
285	
286	\leanok
287	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
288	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
289	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
290	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth}
291	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
292	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
293	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
294	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
295	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
296	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
297	  \(S_M\)-module.
298	\end{lemma}
299	
300	\begin{proof}
301	  The canonical comparison map
302	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
303	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
304	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
305	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
306	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
307	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
308	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
309	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
310	  the conclusion.
311	\end{proof}
312	
313	\begin{lemma}
314	
315	
316	\leanok
317	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
318	  the relative dimension]
319	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
320	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
321	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
322	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
323	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
324	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
325	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
326	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
327	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
328	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
329	\end{lemma}
330	
331	\begin{proof}
332	  Mathlib's
333	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
334	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
335	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
336	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
337	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
338	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
339	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
340	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
341	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
342	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
343	  ranks equal \(n\), so
344	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
345	\end{proof}

347	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
348	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
349	
350	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
351	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
352	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
353	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
354	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
355	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
356	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
357	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
358	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
359	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
360	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
361	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
362	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
363	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
364	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
365	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
366	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
367	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
368	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
369	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
370	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
371	named iter-199+ prover targets.
372	
373	\begin{lemma}
374	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
375	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
376	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
377	        lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal,
378	        lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
379	        lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
380	        lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent,
381	        lem:exists_submersivePresentation_of_standardSmoothReldim}
382	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
383	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
384	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
385	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
386	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
387	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
388	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
389	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
390	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
391	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
392	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
393	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
394	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
395	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
396	  \[
397	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
398	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
399	  \]
400	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
401	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
402	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
403	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
404	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
405	
406	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
407	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
408	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
409	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
410	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
411	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
412	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
413	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
414	\end{lemma}
415	
416	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \texttt{lemma-dimension-prime-polynomial-ring}
417	% we know that $r = \text{trdeg}_k\ \kappa(\mathfrak p)$ is equal to the
418	% dimension of $V(\mathfrak p)$. Pick any maximal chain of primes
419	% $\mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots \subset \mathfrak p_r$
420	% starting with $\mathfrak p$ in $S$. This has length $r$ by Lemma
421	% \texttt{lemma-dimension-spell-it-out}. Let $\mathfrak q_j$, $j \in J$ be
422	% the minimal primes of $S$ which are contained in $\mathfrak p$. These
423	% correspond $1-1$ to minimal primes in $S_{\mathfrak p}$ via the rule
424	% $\mathfrak q_j \mapsto \mathfrak q_jS_{\mathfrak p}$. By Lemma
425	% \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-over-field} we know that
426	% $\dim_x(X)$ is equal to the maximum of the dimensions of the rings
427	% $S/\mathfrak q_j$. For each $j$ pick a maximal chain of primes
428	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
429	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p$. Then $\dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) =
430	% \max_{j \in J} s(j)$. Now, each chain
431	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
432	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots
433	% \subset \mathfrak p_r$ is a maximal chain in $S/\mathfrak q_j$, and by
434	% what was said before we have $\dim_x(X) = \max_{j \in J} r + s(j)$.
435	% The lemma follows."
436	\begin{proof}
437	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
438	        lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal,
439	        lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
440	        lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
441	        lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent,
442	        lem:exists_submersivePresentation_of_standardSmoothReldim}
443	  This is exactly Stacks tag~\texttt{00OE}
444	  (\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}); the proof is a
445	  citation of the dimension theory of finitely generated algebras over a
446	  field. By Noether normalisation the finite-type \(k\)-algebra \(S\) is
447	  finite over a polynomial subalgebra, so the going-up / catenary
448	  dimension theory of such algebras applies. Write
449	  \(r = \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); this transcendence
450	  degree is exactly the dimension of the closed subvariety
451	  \(\overline{\{x\}} = V(\mathfrak q)\). A maximal chain of primes
452	  through \(\mathfrak q\) therefore splits into a
453	  length-\(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) part below \(\mathfrak q\) (recording
454	  the local codimension, i.e.\ the height of \(\mathfrak q\) in the
455	  relevant irreducible component) and a length-\(r\) part above it
456	  (recording the dimension of \(\overline{\{x\}}\)). Taking the maximum
457	  over the minimal primes contained in \(\mathfrak q\) yields
458	  \[
459	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + r
460	    = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q),
461	  \]
462	  which is the asserted formula.
463	
464	  For the geometric specialisation matching the Lean target, take
465	  \(k = \bar k\) algebraically closed and \(S\) standard smooth of
466	  relative dimension \(n\), so that \(\dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\) via
467	  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (the free
468	  K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) has rank \(n\), which
469	  pins the relative dimension). The formula then gives
470	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak
471	  q)\). At a closed point \(\mathfrak q\) is maximal, so by the
472	  Nullstellensatz \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\) and
473	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\); hence
474	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\).
475	\end{proof}
476	
477	\begin{lemma}
478	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
479	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
480	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
481	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
482	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
483	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
484	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
485	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
486	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
487	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
488	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
489	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
490	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
491	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
492	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
493	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\texttt{equation-functorial-omega}),
494	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
495	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
496	  % $S'$-modules
497	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
498	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
499	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
500	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
501	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
502	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
503	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
504	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
505	  % (see Lemma \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
506	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
507	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
508	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
509	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
510	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
511	  \[
512	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
513	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
514	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
515	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
516	  \]
517	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
518	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
519	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
520	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
521	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
522	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
523	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
524	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
525	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
526	  \[
527	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
528	  \]
529	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
530	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
531	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
532	\end{lemma}
533	
534	The Stage 6.B cotangent--K\"ahler chain is realised on the Lean side by the
535	following substrate lemmas, which compute the residue-field tensor finrank and
536	promote the cotangent isomorphism of \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} into
537	the cotangent-space finrank consumed by the regularity criterion.
538	
539	\begin{lemma}\leanok
540	[Stage 6.B (RHS) --- residue-tensor finrank from free rank]
541	  \label{lem:finrank_residue_kaehler_of_free_rank}
542	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.finrank_residueField_tensor_kaehlerDifferential_of_free_rank_eq}
543	  Let \(R \to S_{\mathfrak q}\) be an algebra with \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) a nontrivial
544	  local ring whose K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) are
545	  \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)-free of rank \(n\). Then the residue-field base change
546	  \(\kappa \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) has
547	  \(\kappa\)-dimension \(n\), where \(\kappa\) is the residue field of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
548	\end{lemma}
549	
550	\begin{proof}
551	  Proved directly in Lean: base change preserves rank, and finite rank equals the
552	  natural number \(n\). A sub-step of
553	  \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}.
554	\end{proof}
555	
556	
557	\begin{lemma}[Stage 6.B --- cotangent isomorphism, maximal-ideal form]
558	  \label{lem:cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_kaehler}
559	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
560	  \uses{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}

563	  smooth over \(R\), and \(\Omega_{\kappa/R} = 0\)), there is an
564	  \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)-linear isomorphism between the cotangent module of the maximal
565	  ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and the residue-field base change
566	  \(\kappa \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\). This restates the
567	  kernel-side cotangent isomorphism of \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} with the
568	  canonical maximal-ideal cotangent space as its domain.
569	\end{lemma}
570	
571	\begin{proof}
572	  Proved directly in Lean: transports the isomorphism of
573	  \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} along the equality
574	  \(\ker(S_{\mathfrak q} \to \kappa) = \mathfrak m\).
575	\end{proof}

577	\begin{lemma}\leanok
578	[Stage 6.B --- cotangent-space finrank, formally-smooth residue form]
579	  \label{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}
580	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}
581	  \uses{lem:cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_kaehler, lem:finrank_residue_kaehler_of_free_rank}
582	  With the closed-point hypotheses of

584	  \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) free of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)-rank \(n\), the cotangent

586	\end{lemma}

588	\begin{proof}

594	\begin{lemma}\leanok
595	[Stage 6.B --- cotangent-space finrank at a $\bar k$-rational point]
596	  \label{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue}
597	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue}
598	  \uses{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue}

602	  and the structure map \(R \to \kappa\) to the residue field is bijective (as at a

606

613	\begin{lemma}
614	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
615	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
616	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
617	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
618	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
619	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
620	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
621	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
622	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
623	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
624	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
625	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
626	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
627	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
628	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
629	  dimension equals \(1\).
630	\end{lemma}
631	
632	\begin{proof}
633	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
634	  \begin{enumerate}
635	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
636	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
637	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
638	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
639	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
640	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
641	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
642	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
643	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
644	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
645	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
646	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
647	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
648	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
649	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
650	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
651	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
652	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
653	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
654	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
655	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
656	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
657	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
658	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
659	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
660	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
661	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
662	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
663	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
664	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
665	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
666	      the conclusion.
667	  \end{enumerate}
668	\end{proof}

722	    (preservation of regularity under localisation at a prime;
723	    Stacks \texttt{tag 00OF}) --- \textbf{MISSING at \texttt{b80f227}}
724	    (corrected iter-202: iter-201 Lane COE prover scout confirmed via
725	    \texttt{grep} of \texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/}
726	    that only \texttt{Defs.lean} is present). The
727	    closed-point-to-arbitrary-point alternative route through this
728	    lemma is therefore also gated. The closed-point route does not
729	    need this lemma (see
730	    \cref{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}).
731	\end{itemize}
732	
733	\paragraph{Cascade to consumers.} Closure of Stage 6 (i.e.\ landing
734	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} axiom-clean) collapses the
735	following residual sorries in
736	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}:
737	\begin{itemize}
738	  \item \texttt{isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth} (L434--L526) ---
739	    its final \texttt{sorry} on L526 is filled by chaining the Stage 5a/5b
740	    helpers with the new Stage 6.A/6.B/6.C bridges, exactly as the
741	    proof of \cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} sketches. Closure
742	    is automatic once the three sub-lemmas land axiom-clean.
743	  \item \texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot}
744	    (L547--L599) --- already axiom-clean modulo the named Stacks-00TT
745	    gap; the regularity half is delegated to
746	    \texttt{isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}, so closure
747	    propagates upward without further work.
748	  \item \texttt{localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} (L615--L644) ---
749	    likewise upgrades from gated to axiom-clean automatically: it
750	    consumes \texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot}
751	    and then closes via \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE}.
752	  \item \texttt{extend\_of\_codimOneFree\_of\_smooth} (the body
753	    around L703--L723) --- Step~1 of
754	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof consumes
755	    \texttt{localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} via the valuative
756	    criterion at codim-\(1\) points, so Stage 6 closure unblocks
757	    Step~1 entirely. Step~2 (the depth-\(\geq 2\) local-cohomology
758	    vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}) remains an independent
759	    Mathlib gap and is \emph{not} unblocked by Stage 6.
760	  \item \texttt{indeterminacy\_pure\_codim\_one\_into\_grpScheme}
761	    (L752--L798, the Lean body of
762	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}) --- not directly gated on
763	    Stage 6 (its substantive Mathlib gap is the codim-\(1\)
764	    pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma, Sub-step~4 of
765	    Milne's proof). Stage 6 closure does \emph{not} unblock this
766	    lemma; it is recorded here only for completeness of the
767	    chapter's dependency picture.
768	\end{itemize}
769	
770	The two remaining post-Stage-6 gaps in the chapter are therefore:
771	(a) the Step~2 depth-\(\geq 2\) local-cohomology vanishing
772	(Stacks~\texttt{0AVF}) consumed only by
773	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}, and (b) the
774	function-field-pullback machinery for \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}
775	consumed by \texttt{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} and Sub-step~3 of
776	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}. Neither overlaps with the
777	Stage 6 work, so the three sub-lemmas above are a self-contained
778	mathlib-analogist target.
779	
780	\subsection{Stacks 00OE closed-point substrate (superseded decomposition)}
781	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
782	
783	An earlier decomposition of Stage 6.A
784	(\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) reduced the
785	localised Krull-dimension formula at a closed point to a
786	Jacobian-regular-sequence witness for the relations of a submersive
787	presentation (Stacks 00SW / 00OW together with Matsumura Thm.~14.2). The
788	in-file substrate chain implementing that reduction has been removed as
789	dead code: the closed-point case is now proved unconditionally by the
790	Krull-height-theorem route of \texttt{StandardSmoothDimension.lean} ---
791	full height of maximal ideals of polynomial rings
792	(\cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}), the height
793	lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras
794	(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}), the localised
795	dimension lower bound

text:
Base directory for this skill: /AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

796	(\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), and the
797	cotangent upper bound
798	(\cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}) --- which needs no
799	regular-sequence witness. The Matsumura regular-sequence lemma itself
800	survives as \cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}.
801	The general (non-closed) point case of Stacks 00OE remains open, gated on
802	the localisation-of-regular-local-rings lemma (Stacks \texttt{00OF}),
803	absent from Mathlib.
804	
805	\subsection{Substrate lemmas for Stacks 00OE and the scheme bridges}
806	\label{subsec:codim1_substrate_lemmas}
807	
808	The narrative above is realised on the Lean side by the named substrate lemmas
809	collected here. They feed the Stacks-00OE Krull-dimension formula
810	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} and the regular-stalk assembly.
811	
812	\paragraph{Stacks 00OE localisation/height bridge.}
813	
814	\begin{lemma}\leanok
815	[00OE Step 1 --- localised Krull dimension equals height]
816	  \label{lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}
817	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}
818	  For a prime ideal \(\mathfrak m\) of a commutative ring \(R\) and any localisation
819	  \(A\) of \(R\) at \(\mathfrak m\), the Krull dimension of \(A\) equals the height of
820	  \(\mathfrak m\).
821	\end{lemma}
822	
823	\begin{proof}
824	  Proved directly in Lean: re-export of Mathlib's
825	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}. Consumed by the
826	  closed-point dimension bound
827	  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}.
828	\end{proof}
829	
830	\paragraph{Closed-point form of the 00OE formula (Krull height theorem route).}
831	
832	The following route proves the localised Krull-dimension formula at a maximal
833	ideal unconditionally: the dimension \emph{lower} bound comes from the full
834	height of the pulled-back maximal ideal in the polynomial ring via Krull's
835	height theorem, and the \emph{upper} bound is automatic from the cotangent
836	space. Combined with the
837	cotangent computation \cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue},
838	this proves regularity of a standard-smooth algebra at every closed point.
839	
840	\begin{lemma}\leanok
841	[Maximal ideals of polynomial rings over a field have full height]
842	  \label{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
843	  \lean{MvPolynomial.height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
844	  For a field \(k\) and a finite index set \(I\), every maximal ideal \(M\) of
845	  the polynomial ring \(k[X_i : i \in I]\) has height exactly \(\#I\).
846	\end{lemma}
847	
848	\begin{proof}
849	  Induction on \(\#I\). For \(I = \emptyset\) the ring is the field \(k\), of
850	  Krull dimension \(0\), so every prime has height \(0\). For the induction
851	  step write \(k[X_i : i \in I \sqcup \{\ast\}] = R[X]\) with
852	  \(R = k[X_i : i \in I]\). Given a maximal ideal \(M \subseteq R[X]\), its
853	  contraction \(\mathfrak p = M \cap R\) is again maximal, because \(R\) is a
854	  finitely generated algebra over a field and hence a Jacobson ring. For a
855	  maximal ideal of \(R[X]\) over a Noetherian ring \(R\) one has
856	  \(\operatorname{ht} M = \operatorname{ht} \mathfrak p + 1\): the fibre
857	  \(R[X] \otimes_R \kappa(\mathfrak p) = \kappa(\mathfrak p)[X]\) is a
858	  principal ideal domain, so the image of \(M\) there has height \(1\), and
859	  going-down along the flat extension \(R \to R[X]\) splices the chains. The
860	  claim follows from the inductive hypothesis
861	  \(\operatorname{ht}\mathfrak p = \#I\).
862	\end{proof}
863	
864	\begin{lemma}\leanok
865	[Height lower bound at maximal ideals of standard-smooth algebras]
866	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}
867	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.natCast_le_height_of_isMaximal}
868	  Let \(k\) be a field and \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
869	  dimension \(n\). Then every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) has
870	  height at least \(n\).
871	\end{lemma}
872	
873	\begin{proof}
874	  \uses{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}
875	  Choose a submersive presentation \(S = P/I\) with
876	  \(P = k[X_1, \dots, X_N]\) and \(I = (f_1, \dots, f_c)\) generated by the
877	  \(c\) relations, so that \(n = N - c\); the submersive structure embeds the
878	  relation indices into the generator indices, so \(c \le N\) and
879	  \(n + c = N\). The preimage \(M \subseteq P\) of \(\mathfrak m\) is a maximal
880	  ideal containing \(I\), of height \(N\) by
881	  \cref{lem:mvPolynomial_height_eq_natCard_of_isMaximal}. Krull's height
882	  theorem, in the relative form
883	  \(\operatorname{ht} M \le \operatorname{ht}(M/I) + \mu(I)\) where
884	  \(\mu(I) \le c\) is the minimal number of generators of \(I\), gives
885	  \(N \le \operatorname{ht}(\mathfrak m) + c\) since \(M/I\) corresponds to
886	  \(\mathfrak m\) under \(P/I \simeq S\). Hence
887	  \(n \le \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\).
888	\end{proof}
889	
890	\begin{lemma}\leanok
891	[Dimension lower bound at closed points of standard-smooth algebras]
892	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}
893	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.le_ringKrullDim_of_isLocalization_atPrime}
894	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra of relative
895	  dimension \(n\), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and
896	  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\). Then
897	  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\).
898	\end{lemma}
899	
900	\begin{proof}
901	  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal,
902	        lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}
903	  Combine \(\dim S_{\mathfrak m} = \operatorname{ht}\mathfrak m\)
904	  (\cref{lem:ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime}) with the height
905	  lower bound \cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_height_of_isMaximal}.
906	\end{proof}
907	
908	\begin{lemma}\leanok
909	[Regularity from the cotangent upper bound]
910	  \label{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}
911	  \lean{IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim}
912	  Let \(A\) be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and
913	  residue field \(\kappa\). If
914	  \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 \le \dim A\), then \(A\) is a
915	  regular local ring.
916	\end{lemma}
917	
918	\begin{proof}
919	  By Nakayama's lemma the minimal number of generators \(\mu(\mathfrak m)\)
920	  equals \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2\), and Krull's height theorem
921	  gives \(\dim A \le \mu(\mathfrak m)\) in any Noetherian local ring. The
922	  hypothesis forces \(\mu(\mathfrak m) = \dim A\), which is the definition of
923	  regularity.
924	\end{proof}
925	
926	\begin{lemma}\leanok
927	[Stacks 00TT at a rational closed point, algebra level]
928	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
929	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_of_bijective_residue}
930	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(S\) a standard-smooth \(k\)-algebra,
931	  \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\) a maximal ideal, and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) a
932	  localisation of \(S\) at \(\mathfrak m\) such that the composite
933	  \(k \to S_{\mathfrak m} \to \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) is bijective (a
934	  \(k\)-rational point). Then \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is a regular local ring.
935	\end{lemma}
936	
937	\begin{proof}
938	  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization,
939	        lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim,
940	        lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue,
941	        lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth,
942	        lem:module_free_kaehler_localization,
943	        lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
944	        lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
945	  Let \(n\) be the relative dimension of \(S\) over \(k\)
946	  (\cref{lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}).
947	  The ring \(S\) is Noetherian (finite type over a field), hence so is
948	  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\), and \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is formally smooth over \(k\)
949	  (a localisation of a smooth algebra). Its K\"ahler module
950	  \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak m}/k}\) is free of rank \(n\)
951	  (\cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_standardSmooth},
952	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
953	  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}), so by the conormal
954	  computation at the \(k\)-rational point
955	  (\cref{lem:finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_residue})
956	  \(\dim_{\kappa} \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 = n\). Since
957	  \(n \le \dim S_{\mathfrak m}\)
958	  (\cref{lem:standard_smooth_le_ringKrullDim_localization}), regularity
959	  follows from \cref{lem:regular_of_finrank_cotangent_le_dim}.
960	\end{proof}
961	
962	\begin{lemma}\leanok
963	[Stacks 00TT at closed points over an algebraically closed field]
964	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed}
965	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing_localizationAtPrime_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed}
966	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field and \(S\) a standard-smooth
967	  \(\bar k\)-algebra. Then for every maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m \subseteq S\)
968	  the local ring \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is regular.
969	\end{lemma}
970	
971	\begin{proof}
972	  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}
973	  By Zariski's lemma the residue field \(S/\mathfrak m\) is a finite, hence
974	  algebraic, extension of \(\bar k\); as \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed,
975	  \(\bar k \to S/\mathfrak m\) is bijective, and composing with the
976	  isomorphism \(S/\mathfrak m \simeq \kappa(\mathfrak m)\) (valid since
977	  \(\mathfrak m\) is maximal) shows every maximal ideal is a
978	  \(\bar k\)-rational point. Conclude with
979	  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_rational_point}.
980	\end{proof}
981	
982	\begin{lemma}\leanok
983	[Step B.e --- standard-smooth algebras over an algebraically closed field are reduced]
984	  \label{lem:standard_smooth_reduced_algclosed}
985	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isReduced_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed}
986	  \uses{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed,
987	        lem:isDomain_of_regularLocal}
988	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field and \(S\) a standard-smooth
989	  \(\bar k\)-algebra. Then \(S\) is reduced.
990	\end{lemma}
991	
992	\begin{proof}
993	  If \(S\) is the zero ring the claim is vacuous, so assume \(S \neq 0\).
994	  Reducedness is detected at localisations at maximal ideals: if \(x \in S\) is
995	  nilpotent and nonzero, its annihilator is a proper ideal, hence contained in
996	  some maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\); the image of \(x\) in \(S_{\mathfrak m}\)
997	  is then nilpotent and nonzero, so \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is not reduced. It
998	  therefore suffices to show every \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is reduced. By
999	  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_regular_at_closed_point_algclosed} the local ring
1000	  \(S_{\mathfrak m}\) is regular, by \cref{lem:isDomain_of_regularLocal} it is
1001	  an integral domain, and integral domains are reduced.
1002	\end{proof}
1003	
1004	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1005	[Step B.f --- a scheme smooth over an algebraically closed field is reduced]
1006	  \label{lem:smooth_scheme_reduced_algclosed}
1007	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed}
1008	  \uses{lem:exists_standardSmooth_at_of_smooth, lem:gammaSpecField_ringEquiv,
1009	        lem:isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen,
1010	        lem:standard_smooth_reduced_algclosed}
1011	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field and let \(X\) be a scheme with
1012	  a smooth morphism \(X \to \Spec(\bar k)\). Then \(X\) is reduced.
1013	\end{lemma}
1014	
1015	\begin{proof}
1016	  A scheme is reduced if and only if all of its stalks are reduced, so fix
1017	  \(z \in X\). By \cref{lem:exists_standardSmooth_at_of_smooth} there are affine
1018	  opens \(U \subseteq \Spec(\bar k)\) and \(V \ni z\) with \(V\) mapping into
1019	  \(U\) such that \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)\) is standard
1020	  smooth. Since \(U\) contains the image of \(z\) it is nonempty, so
1021	  \cref{lem:gammaSpecField_ringEquiv} identifies
1022	  \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, U) \simeq \bar k\); transporting the presentation along
1023	  this isomorphism makes \(\Gamma(X, V)\) a standard-smooth
1024	  \(\bar k\)-algebra, which is reduced by
1025	  \cref{lem:standard_smooth_reduced_algclosed}. The stalk
1026	  \(\mathcal O_{X,z}\) is a localisation of \(\Gamma(X, V)\) by
1027	  \cref{lem:isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen}, and a localisation of
1028	  a reduced ring is reduced.
1029	\end{proof}
1030	
1031	\paragraph{Relative dimension and scheme-to-algebra bridges.}
1032	
1033	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1034	[Stage 6 sub-gap (i) --- relative dimension of a standard-smooth algebra]
1035	  \label{lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
1036	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth}
1037	  Every standard-smooth \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is standard smooth of some specific
1038	  relative dimension \(n \in \mathbb N\).
1039	\end{lemma}
1040	
1041	\begin{proof}
1042	  Proved directly in Lean: read the dimension off the underlying submersive
1043	  presentation. A sub-step of \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}.
1044	\end{proof}
1045	
1046	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1047	[Step B.a --- submersive presentation of a relative-dimension-$n$ algebra]
1048	  \label{lem:exists_submersivePresentation_of_standardSmoothReldim}
1049	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}
1050	  \uses{lem:exists_standardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_standardSmooth}
1051	  An \(R\)-algebra \(S\) that is standard smooth of relative dimension \(n\) admits an
1052	  explicit submersive presentation \(P\) with \(P.\mathrm{dimension} = n\).
1053	\end{lemma}
1054	
1055	\begin{proof}
1056	  Proved directly in Lean: re-export of the underlying-presentation field of the
1057	  relative-dimension instance.
1058	\end{proof}
1059	
1060	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1061	[Step B.b --- affine-open stalk is a localisation]
1062	  \label{lem:isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen}
1063	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen}
1064	  For an affine open \(V \ni z\) of a scheme \(X\), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X,z}\) is
1065	  the localisation of the section ring \(\Gamma(X, V)\) at the prime ideal of \(z\).
1066	\end{lemma}
1067	
1068	\begin{proof}
1069	  Proved directly in Lean: re-export of \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}.
1070	\end{proof}
1071	
1072	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1073	[Step B.c helper --- nonempty open on a one-point space is everything]
1074	  \label{lem:open_eq_top_of_subsingleton}
1075	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.open_eq_top_of_subsingleton}
1076	  On a scheme whose underlying space is a subsingleton (at most one point), every
1077	  nonempty open subset equals the whole space.
1078	\end{lemma}
1079	
1080	\begin{proof}
1081	  Proved directly in Lean: the unique-point argument. A sub-step of
1082	  \cref{lem:gammaSpecField_ringEquiv}.
1083	\end{proof}
1084	
1085	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1086	[Step B.c --- sections of $\Spec \bar k$ over a nonempty open are $\bar k$]
1087	  \label{lem:gammaSpecField_ringEquiv}
1088	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.gammaSpecField_ringEquiv}
1089	  \uses{lem:open_eq_top_of_subsingleton}
1090	  For a field \(\bar k\) and any nonempty open \(U\) of \(\Spec(\bar k)\), the section
1091	  ring \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, U)\) is ring-isomorphic to \(\bar k\). This gives the
1092	  algebraically-closed base ring of the standard-smooth presentation a clean
1093	  \(\bar k\)-identification.
1094	\end{lemma}
1095	
1096	\begin{proof}
1097	  Proved directly in Lean: \(\Spec(\bar k)\) has a single point, so
1098	  \cref{lem:open_eq_top_of_subsingleton} forces \(U = \top\) and the sections collapse
1099	  to the global sections \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k, \top) \simeq \bar k\).
1100	\end{proof}

1113	\begin{proof}

1115	  the right-unitor and promote scalars along the surjective quotient map. A sub-step of
1116	  \cref{lem:isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring}.
1117	\end{proof}

1123	  \uses{lem:quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv}
1124	  Let \(r \in A\) be such that multiplication by \(r\) is injective on \(A\), and
1125	  suppose the images of a list \(rs\) form a regular sequence over the ring quotient
1126	  \(A/(r)\). Then \(r :: rs\) is a regular sequence over \(A\).
1127	\end{lemma}
1128	
1129	\begin{proof}

1132	  \cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}.
1133	\end{proof}

1135	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1136	[Step A1 --- cotangent independence descends to the quotient]
1137	  \label{lem:matsumura_descent_cotangent}
1138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.matsumura_descent_cotangent}
1139	  Let \((A, \mathfrak m)\) be a local ring and \(f_0,\dots,f_m \in \mathfrak m\) with
1140	  \(\kappa(A)\)-linearly independent cotangent classes. Then the tail
1141	  \(f_1,\dots,f_m\), pushed into the quotient \(A' = A/(f_0)\), has
1142	  \(\kappa(A')\)-linearly independent cotangent classes in
1143	  \(\mathfrak m'/\mathfrak m'^2\).
1144	\end{lemma}
1145	
1146	\begin{proof}
1147	  Proved directly in Lean: the cotangent map \(A \to A'\) is surjective with kernel
1148	  spanned by the class of \(f_0\); a kernel-disjointness argument reduces a
1149	  \(\kappa(A')\)-relation to a \(\kappa(A)\)-relation killed by the full independence
1150	  hypothesis. A sub-step of
1151	  \cref{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}.
1152	\end{proof}

1154	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1155	[Step A1 --- Matsumura: cotangent-independent elements form a regular sequence]
1156	  \label{lem:matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent}

1158	  \uses{lem:matsumura_descent_cotangent, lem:isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring}
1159	  Let \((A, \mathfrak m)\) be a Noetherian regular local ring and \(f_1,\dots,f_n \in
1160	  \mathfrak m\) whose cotangent classes in \(\mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2\) are
1161	  \(\kappa(A)\)-linearly independent. Then \(f_1,\dots,f_n\) form a regular sequence in
1162	  \(A\) (Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Thm.~14.2; Stacks 00NQ).
1163	\end{lemma}
1164	
1165	\begin{proof}
1166	  Proved directly in Lean: induction on \(n\). The head \(f_1\) is a non-zero-divisor
1167	  (a regular local ring is a domain), the cotangent independence descends to the
1168	  quotient \(A/(f_1)\) by \cref{lem:matsumura_descent_cotangent}, and the
1169	  ring-quotient cons rule \cref{lem:isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring} reassembles the
1170	  regular sequence.
1171	\end{proof}
